creator: Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) Microsoft lays off nearly 5,000 employees across Xbox, commercial sales
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
content: Microsoft cut around 4,800 roles, or 2.1% of its global workforce, on Monday — the latest in a series of layoffs that’s stoking fears of AI replacing jobs. The layoffs will hit Xbox and commercial sales the hardest.
contentSnippet: Microsoft cut around 4,800 roles, or 2.1% of its global workforce, on Monday — the latest in a series of layoffs that’s stoking fears of AI replacing jobs. The layoffs will hit Xbox and commercial sales the hardest.

creator: Anna Heim
title: (No AI) Station F ramps up as a launchpad for Europe’s hottest AI startups
dc:creator: Anna Heim
content: Station F, a Paris-based startup hub founded by French billionaire Xavier Niel, is gearing up for a new edition of its F/ai accelerator program in a bid to strengthen its positioning as a stepping stone for promising AI startups.
contentSnippet: Station F, a Paris-based startup hub founded by French billionaire Xavier Niel, is gearing up for a new edition of its F/ai accelerator program in a bid to strengthen its positioning as a stepping stone for promising AI startups.

title: (No AI) Microsoft is laying off 4,800 employees
author: Tom Warren
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/gettyimages-2262967364.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Satya Nadella in February 2026. | Photo by Sven Hoppe / picture alliance via Getty Images

A year after cutting around [9,100 employees](https://www.theverge.com/news/693535/microsoft-layoffs-july-2025-xbox), Microsoft is making further layoffs today as it begins its new financial year. The software maker is laying off around 4,800 employees today, approximately 2.1 percent of its workforce. Most of the employees affected by today's cuts are in Microsoft's commercial sales business or the company's Xbox division.

In an internal memo to employees, Amy Coleman, executive vice president and Microsoft's chief people officer, blamed the job losses on a changing technology industry and the "need to adjust resources and roles and shift how we operate" to respond to how AI is impacting companies like Microsof …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/news/961528/microsoft-layoffs-july-2026-sales-xbox)

title: (No AI) Some of the nation’s rich are letting AI teach their kids
author: Terrence O’Brien
content:
![Photo collage of a pixelated student at a desk.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/25584700/STK483_EDUCATION_C.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Most Americans [don't trust AI](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/644853/pew-gallup-data-americans-dont-trust-ai). It's proven that it doesn't know what [safe toppings](https://www.theverge.com/2024/5/30/24168344/google-defends-ai-overviews-search-results) for pizza are. People don't even want to listen to [AI music](https://www.theverge.com/entertainment/915027/deezer-ai-music-daily-uploads). But none of that matters for some of America's wealthy, who are turning to AI to teach their kids instead of traditional schools.

Companies like Forge Prep and Alpha School are charging families tens of thousands of dollars to turn their kids into beta testers for AI tutors and "interactive project-based workshops." Unsurprisingly, Silicon Valley have been major adopters of this new model. Shaun Johnson, a San Francisco-based venture capitalist, told [_The Wall Street Journal_](https://www.wsj.com/us-news/education/alternative-education-wealthy-families-ai-cd7922b3?st=TeME61&reflink=desktopwebshare_permalink) that he plans to send his …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/961505/wealthy-ai-schools-alpha-forge-prep)

title: (No AI) How ChatGPT adoption has expanded
content: New OpenAI Signals data shows how ChatGPT adoption is growing globally, with users increasing usage, exploring more capabilities, and driving growth across regions and languages.
contentSnippet: New OpenAI Signals data shows how ChatGPT adoption is growing globally, with users increasing usage, exploring more capabilities, and driving growth across regions and languages.

creator: Joel Khalili
title: (No AI) Google DeepMind Unionization Talks Are Off to a Rocky Start
dc:creator: Joel Khalili
content: During negotiations on Wednesday, employees voiced frustrations with what they consider an unwillingness among executives to engage meaningfully with the prospect of unionization.
contentSnippet: During negotiations on Wednesday, employees voiced frustrations with what they consider an unwillingness among executives to engage meaningfully with the prospect of unionization.

creator: Maxwell Zeff
title: (No AI) Can Cursor Remain a Platform for OpenAI and Anthropic’s Models Inside SpaceX?
dc:creator: Maxwell Zeff
content: Cursor hopes to continue offering third-party AI models after it's acquired by SpaceX, testing the relationships between frontier AI labs.
contentSnippet: Cursor hopes to continue offering third-party AI models after it's acquired by SpaceX, testing the relationships between frontier AI labs.
